exemplar.title: How Various Tin sizes and shapes Affects the rate of growth of a cake batter

detailedExemplarData.title: How Various Tin sizes and shapes Affects the rate of growth of a cake batter

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and logical narrative structure (introduction â methodology â analysis â conclusion) aids reader comprehension
Wellâorganized sections with headings and numbered lists provide effective signâposting
Tables and figures are embedded next to relevant text, supporting flow of ideas
weaknesses: Occasional digressions on theory interrupt the logical flow
Inconsistent appendix references and minor duplication reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (coherence and organization) clearly meet bandâ3 criteria, showing a coherent and wellâstructured exploration. Conciseness falls slightly below the top level, with unnecessary repetitions and digressions, placing this criterion at the upper bandâ3 (scoreâ3).

strengths: Effective use of tables, scatter plots and fitted functions to visualize data
Summation table transparently displays intermediate values for correlation calculation
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation (e.g. â1/xâ for a mean, lowercase units) and missing units in tables and axes
Transcription errors and misâtyped symbols reduce clarity
Blurry images and unlabeled axes limit the precision of representations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student employs appropriate mathematical language and multiple representations, recurrent notation lapses and clarity issues prevent a mostly consistent or excellent communication. This places the work solidly in bandâ2 (scoreâ2).

strengths: Independent redesign of the experiment after initial failure shows clear independent thinking
Personal narrative (interest in baking, rationale for tin choice) demonstrates strong personal engagement
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows textbook methods with limited novel insight
Creativity is confined to topic choice rather than methodological innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Significant evidence of independent thinking and a genuine personal approach earn bandâ2, but the limited creativity and initiative prevent reaching the highest level, so the score is 2 out of 3.

strengths: Identification of key sources of experimental error shows meaningful reflection
Reflection on the interaction between mathematical and experimental processes demonstrates some depth
weaknesses: Critical analysis remains basic, largely restating correlation results without deeper statistical critique
Evaluation of outcomes is superficial, with few quantitative or forwardâlooking suggestions
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration contains some meaningful reflection but only basic critical analysis and limited evaluation of outcomes, aligning with bandâ1 (scoreâ1).

strengths: Relevant mathematical models (linear, exponential, Pearsonâs r) are chosen appropriately
Correct statement of the Pearson correlation formula and clear data tables enhance understanding
weaknesses: Arithmetic and transcription errors in gradient and mean calculations reduce accuracy
Omission of intermediate values and absence of metrics (e.g. residuals or rÂ²) limit the rigor of the application
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the mathematics used is entirely relevant, recurring minor errors and incomplete procedural details constrain the work to bandâ2 (scoreâ2) rather than full bandâ3 performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: **Explanations of calculations follow a logical sequence, but clarity is uneven.** For instance, the mean is mis-typed as â1/xâ, and the derivation of exponential parameters contains transcription errors. These problems limit overall clarity to âsome logical structureâ, hence band 2.

**Calculations are mostly correct and steps are shown, demonstrating good understanding; however, minor inaccuracies occur** (e.g. mis-labelled means, rounding inconsistencies). These are âminor errorsâ compatible with band 3 (âgood understanding and mostly correctâ).

**Tables, scatter plots and fitted functions are provided for each tin, alongside a summary overlay graph.** Together with descriptive text they allow the reader to visualize relationships effectively, so representation is âeffectiveâ (band 3). Minor issues (blurry images, axes without labels) prevent the highest band.

**The investigation follows a recognizable narrative (introduction â methodology â analysis â evaluation â conclusion) and ideas generally progress in a sensible order.** The student explains the first failed attempt, justifies changes and then analyses the improved data, which helps the reader to follow the logic. However, the argument occasionally meanders (e.g. long digressions on Pearsonâsâr theory) and some conclusions appear before the supporting calculations, so the development cannot be described as âexcellentâ. Overall coherence is solid but not exemplary, meriting band 3.

**The work employs basic mathematical tools in a non-standard baking context, but creativity is limited to the choice of topic.** Modelling techniques are textbook and no novel mathematical insight is introduced, so only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is evident (band 1).

**Critical analysis is present but basic.** The student mainly restates what the correlation coefficients imply without deeper statistical discussion or consideration of the validity of using such small data sets, so this remains at the âbasic analysisâ level (band 1).

**Outcomes are discussed superficially; there is acknowledgement of errors but little quantitative assessment of their impact or suggestions for future modelling.** This is better than no evaluation, yet not âclearâ or âcomprehensiveâ, so band 1 fits.

**The evaluation section recognises several practical sources of error (temperature loss, tin placement, timing accuracy) and relates them to the unexpected linear trend.** This constitutes meaningful, though not profound, reflection, aligning with band 2.

**Standard mathematical symbols ( m, b, r, Î£ ) and equation forms are used, but notation is sometimes imprecise** â e.g. â1/xâ17.754â for a mean, inconsistent use of uppercase/lowercase, and missing units. These lapses prevent âmostly consistentâ usage, yet appropriate language is present often enough for band 2.

**A clear personal narrative (lifelong interest in baking, rationale for tin choice, reflection on methodology) shows that the approach is genuinely the studentâs.** While not highly innovative, it is more than âsome evidenceâ, so band 2 is appropriate.

**Much of the 3 000-word text is relevant, yet there are noticeable digressions** (lengthy re-explanations of basic formulae, repeated definitions of variables, screenshots that add little information). These additions reduce conciseness, and the word count exceeds IB guidance. Consequently the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ, corresponding to band 2.

**Linear functions, simple exponential models and Pearsonâs correlation are all relevant to rate-of-rise data, and they are applied correctly to each tin.** Although elementary, this meets the description ârelevant mathematics used appropriatelyâ (band 3).

**The student independently designs a second, improved experiment after reflecting on the failure of the first, chooses which model (linear vs exponential) to adopt, and calculates Pearsonâs r by hand.** These choices demonstrate âsignificant evidence of independent thinkingâ, placing the strand in band 2.

**Sections, sub-headings and numbered lists provide clear sign-posting, and tables/figures are embedded logically next to the relevant text.** Introduction, methodology, analysis and evaluation are distinct and easy to locate. Minor weaknesses include inconsistent appendix references and occasional duplication, but the exploration is âwell organised with clear structureâ, fitting band 3.

**All mathematical content sits squarely at SL levelâstraight-line graphs, exponential growth, Pearsonâs-râwhich is âcommensurate with the courseâ.** Complexity is modest, so band 3 is appropriate (not 4+ which require fully/ comprehensively applied mathematics).

annotations.comment: Evaluation of the unsuccessful trial thoughtfully identifies key sources of error, demonstrating meaningful reflection on methodology challenges.
Bake time specified as â50.01 minutesâ suggests overprecision; round to a practical value (e.g., 50 min) and include units consistently.
Temperature annotation â210Â°câ uses lowercase âcâ; standardize to â210 Â°Câ to align with SI notation.
The improved-trial data table is well laid out, showing clear comparative data across tin sizes.
The text refers to axes T and R without defining them in the caption; explicitly label axes as time (min) and rise (mm).
The gradient calculation uses points (36.14,20) and (17.03,10) but the resulting m â0.520 conflicts with the quotient ~0.523; re-check arithmetic.
Exponent difference uses x=17.02 in one section and x=17.03 elsewhere; ensure consistency of data points in all calculations.
The presentation of a and b values in the exponential model is correct and shows good understanding of simultaneous equations.
Best-fit discussion describes visual fit but lacks quantitative metrics (e.g. residuals or $$r^2$$); include these to strengthen justification.
Figure 8 for medium tin lacks axis labels and scale markers; adding these would make the comparison clearer.
The title is clear and descriptive, immediately informing the reader of the investigation focus on tin size, shape, and cake rise.
The inclusion of word count and student number adds administrative clutter; consider relocating these details to a cover sheet or appendix.
The introductory paragraph effectively uses personal narrative to engage the reader and demonstrate genuine interest in the topic.
Aim section repeats similar statements and could be more concise; refine wording to focus on one clear research question.
Definitions of linear and exponential variables use inconsistent notation and lack units; standardize symbols and specify units where applicable.
The first-attempt method list lacks details on repeated trials and controls; include number of repetitions and control variables for reliability.
Figure 1 provides a helpful visual of the setup, aiding reader comprehension of how measurements were taken.
The data table lacks axis labels and units; add clear labels (e.g., time in minutes, rise in mm) to enhance clarity.
Interpretation of $$r\approx0.160$$ treats any positive as meaningful; discuss the low magnitude and its statistical insignificance.
The interpretation in the combined plot claims tin shape affects rate but earlier correlation results show weak relationships for medium tin; reconcile this inconsistency.
The Pearson correlation formula is stated accurately using correct summation notation, aiding clarity for statistical analysis.
The calculation label â1/x â17.754â should read $$\overline{x}\approx17.754$$; correct this notation to avoid confusion.
Summation table demonstrates all intermediate values clearly, supporting transparency of the correlation calculation.
Square-root step shows $$\sqrt{908.62\times250}$$ but intermediate product value is omitted; include it (e.g. \(908.62\times250=227155\)).
Reflection on personal learning demonstrates awareness of how mathematical and experimental processes interact, enhancing depth of insight.
Bibliography entries are present but formatting is inconsistent; use a standard citation style (e.g., APA) and ensure uniformity.
The evaluation section comprehensively discusses practical errors (oven door, positioning, timing) demonstrating thoughtful critical analysis.
